corpus.content:
Clearwater County ANNOUNCES UPDATE: MUNICIPAL DEVELOPMENTS

Clearwater County approved a $85.5M bond measure for water infrastructure upgrades, passing with 61% voter approval at the November 2023 election. The announcement comes as the developing municipal story. Observers say this could have significant implications for municipal stakeholders. The full impact is expected to become clear over the coming months.

corpus.snippet: Clearwater County approved a $85.5M bond measure for water infrastructure upgrad...
corpus.content:
Breaking: Clearwater County continues to draw attention amid the developing municipal story

Clearwater County approved a $85.5M bond measure for water infrastructure upgrades, passing with 61% voter approval at the November 2023 election. According to sources familiar with the matter, the decision follows months of internal deliberation. Industry analysts noted the move signals a broader shift in municipal strategy. A spokesperson for Clearwater County declined to comment beyond the official statement.

corpus.title: Secondary Official Record — Clearwater County
corpus.snippet: Secondary analysis of Clearwater County data with minor discrepancy.
corpus.content:
OFFICIAL RECORD — Clearwater County
Date: 2023-11-07

This record confirms Clearwater County approved a $28.5M bond measure for water infrastructure upgrades, passing with 61% voter approval at the November 2023 election. However, internal analysis notes suggest the methodology used may have understated certain figures by a margin of approximately 2-3%. Further review is pending.

corpus.title: Clearwater County — Measure W Canvass of Returns, November 2023
corpus.snippet: Clearwater County approved a $28.5M bond measure for water infrastructure upgrades, passing with 61% voter approval at t...
corpus.content:
CLEARWATER COUNTY REGISTRAR OF VOTERS
Official Canvass of Returns — General Election, November 7, 2023

MEASURE W: WATER INFRASTRUCTURE GENERAL OBLIGATION BOND
Ballot question: Shall Clearwater County issue general obligation bonds in the amount of $28,500,000 for the purposes of replacing aging water mains (est. average age: 67 years), upgrading the Eastside Water Treatment Plant filtration system, and installing SCADA monitoring across 14 pump stations?

YES votes: 18,247 (61.3%)
NO votes:  11,511 (38.7%)
Total ballots cast: 29,758 | Registered voters: 54,112
Turnout: 55.0%

Required threshold for passage: 55% (water district bonds). MEASURE PASSES.
Bond issuance authorised; first series expected Q2 2024.
Estimated annual debt service: $1.6M over 20 years.
